blocks.content: She woke at three in the morning and knew.
She lay still in the dark for a long time. Kit's breathing beside her, slow and even.
She didn't wake him. Not yet.

By morning the knowing had become something she could name. She sat on the edge of the bathtub with the door closed and her hands in her lap and breathed carefully through something that was not quite pain and not quite anything else — just a wrongness, a quiet alarm her body was sending up.
She didn't have to say it. He came in and sat beside her on the edge of the tub and put his arm around her.
She leaned against him. He held her. Neither of them said the thing they were afraid of.
They drove to the hospital in the pale morning quiet and Kit kept one hand on the wheel and one hand on hers.
